page: Amazon Web Services m5n.4xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 16 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) derived from 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, supported by a 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, meaning storage relies entirely on external EBS volumes.

Performance benchmarks position the m5n.4xlarge primarily in the average tier for multi-core and memory operations. While single-core performance tests, such as stress-ng single-core and gzip compression, fall into the bottom 25% category, multi-core execution in stress-ng and Geekbench registers in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also demonstrate average performance. The instance achieves average ratings in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput, alongside average text and image processing capabilities.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5n.4xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring steady multi-threaded processing and high network throughput without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps supports data-intensive transfers. This hardware profile is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, database operations, software compilation, and light language model inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.4xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 16 vCPUs (8 physical cores) running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance is relatively weak. With a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps, it provides a balanced resource profile. This instance is suitable for general-purpose applications, static web serving, software compilation, and standard database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.4xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering balanced multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 vCPUs, 64.0 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance offering dedicated Intel Xeon processing, optimized network bandwidth, and steady multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.

64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network transfers
Average multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU hardware bundled
Suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and database operations
